title: Tempting Twice Cooked French Cheese Tartiflette
description: A sour and indulgent French dish featuring a cheese pull, this tartiflette is a low-calorie-friendly twist on a classic Basque-French recipe, cooked to perfection in a cast-iron skillet with canola oil and double-fry KFC techniques.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large cast-iron skillet, heat the canola oil over medium-high heat.
Add the diced smoked bacon and cook until crispy, then set aside.
Add the chopped onions to the skillet and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the diced potatoes to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the sliced Reblochon cheese, chopped fresh thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cooked potatoes, onions, and garlic to the bowl with the cheese mixture and stir until well combined.
Add the cooked smoked bacon to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to the cast-iron skillet and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
